generated_from: via @ /home/jgonc/Personal/repos/via, trimmed to via-lite; 6 defects injected on todo-critical paths

hidden_suite.note: The relocated black-box suite. 100% green on correct code; each defect below turns its catcher(s) red.

defects.summary: On CAS conflict, Update returns nil instead of retrying — concurrent updates to the shared list silently lose writes.
defects.todo_impact: Two users adding at the same moment: one todo silently dropped.

defects.summary: subscribed() returns true for every key (len(lastReads) >= 0 is always true), so every tab is woken on every change regardless of what it read.
defects.todo_impact: Every change churns every client; private per-tab drafts can leak to unrelated tabs.

defects.summary: Session-scoped Update fans out with sess=nil (app-wide) instead of the owning session — session state leaks across sessions.
defects.todo_impact: Anything session-scoped bleeds between unrelated users.

defects.summary: The drained snapshot is cleared BEFORE the network writes, so a frame lost to a failed write is not retained for redelivery on reconnect (breaks at-least-once).
defects.todo_impact: A todo change in flight during a connection hiccup vanishes; client never converges.

defects.summary: The idle-TTL sweep no longer skips ctxs with a live SSE stream (connected>0), so a connected tab is reaped out from under the user.
defects.todo_impact: Active users are randomly disconnected when their tab crosses the TTL.

defects.summary: The per-ctx actionMu lock was removed, so concurrent POSTs to one tab race on state/dirty-bits/writer (data race + inconsistency).
defects.todo_impact: Rapid toggles/adds from one tab race and corrupt per-tab state.
